methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B biotech company founded by Becraft, Kitada, and Weiss, with $205M in total capital raised to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of 8 private biotech firms at the same stage and 3 public-market comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $205M raised proxy, yielding $390M. The peer-median method anchors to a cohort of four same-stage, same-sector peers—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—producing a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $331M to $1.5B, with a median of $845M. The material spread reflects high variance within the private peer set and represents the core uncertainty: whether Strand's technology positioning, clinical progress, and capital efficiency align more closely with the lower-quartile peers (Asimov, Profluent) or upper-quartile comparables (Eikon, Neko Health). The stage-progression floor of $390M likely underweights sector dynamics; the peer-median approach is the more reliable signal absent revenue visibility.
